message:
perf(btree): lazily populate cell slot cache

Parse only the requested cell on a cell-slot cache miss instead of pre-parsing every cell on the page. Keep the cache keyed by page number and mutation counter, but store sparse per-cell slots so point probes do not pay for unrelated cells.

Also fixes small rustfmt/clippy drift that blocked the required workspace gates.

Perf: MT 8t profile after the change drops BtCursor::parse_cell_at and CellRef::parse out of the >0.5% report; throughput remains roughly flat at 5.5k wps.

Verification: cargo fmt --all --check; git diff --cached --check; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_beigemouse cargo check --workspace --all-targets; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_beigemouse cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_beigemouse cargo test -p fsqlite-btree cell_slot_cache -- --nocapture. UBS was run on the staged files and exited nonzero on pre-existing file-level panic/unwrap/security-pattern inventory, while its embedded fmt/clippy/check/test-build sections were clean.
